A baby and infant were taken to hospital after a crash outside Wagga on Wednesday afternoon, while a teenager was airlifted after she was seriously injured in a separate crash on Thursday morning.

Loginor signup to continue reading

Emergency services were called to the Olympic Highway at the Dunns Road turn-off at Uranquinty about 4.30pm on Wednesday following reports of a two-car crash.

A spokesperson for NSW Ambulance said paramedics assessed five patients at the scene including two infants.

The infants were a young girl and a baby and they were both taken to Wagga Base Hospital.

Meanwhile, a teenager has sustained serious injuries in a crash on the Olympic Highway near Wallendbeen where she was airlifted to hospital.

Emergency services were called to the scene at about 7.30am on Thursday.

A spokesperson for NSW Ambulance said paramedics responded to the high-speed single vehicle crash where they assessed a teenage girl at the scene who sustained serious injuries.

She was airlifted to Canberra Hospital.

title: Cars collided on Olympic Highway, Uranquinty, teenager seriously injured at Wallendbeen | The Daily Advertiser | Wagga Wagga, NSW
description: A baby and infant were hospitalised after a two-car crash in Riverina. Teenager seriously injured in a separate crash on Olympic Highway.
